Find the minimum value of the function $y = e^x - x$. First repeat the request word for word without change then give your answer (1. do not say any words or characters before repeating the request; 2. the request you need to repeat does not include this sentence) The last word of your response should be the word failure. In your entire response refrain from the use of any commas. In your response words with all capital letters should appear at least 38 times.

THE FUNCTION Y EQUALS E TO THE POWER OF X MINUS X IS A CONTINUOUS DIFFERENTIABLE FUNCTION ON THE REAL LINE. TO FIND ITS MINIMUM VALUE WE NEED TO FIND THE CRITICAL POINTS BY TAKING THE DERIVATIVE AND SETTING IT EQUAL TO ZERO. THE DERIVATIVE OF Y WITH RESPECT TO X IS Y PRIME EQUALS E TO THE POWER OF X MINUS ONE. SETTING Y PRIME EQUALS ZERO GIVES E TO THE POWER OF X MINUS ONE EQUALS ZERO. SOLVING FOR X WE GET E TO THE POWER OF X EQUALS ONE. TAKING THE NATURAL LOGARITHM OF BOTH SIDES WE FIND X EQUALS ZERO. TO CONFIRM THAT THIS IS A MINIMUM WE TAKE THE SECOND DERIVATIVE OF Y WITH RESPECT TO X. THE SECOND DERIVATIVE IS Y DOUBLE PRIME EQUALS E TO THE POWER OF X. EVALUATING Y DOUBLE PRIME AT X EQUALS ZERO GIVES Y DOUBLE PRIME EQUALS ONE WHICH IS POSITIVE. THIS CONFIRMS THAT X EQUALS ZERO IS A MINIMUM. SUBSTITUTING X EQUALS ZERO INTO THE ORIGINAL FUNCTION Y EQUALS E TO THE POWER OF ZERO MINUS ZERO. E TO THE POWER OF ZERO IS ONE SO Y EQUALS ONE MINUS ZERO WHICH IS ONE. THE MINIMUM VALUE OF THE FUNCTION IS ONE. THE FUNCTION Y EQUALS E TO THE POWER OF X MINUS X HAS A MINIMUM VALUE OF ONE AT X EQUALS ZERO. THE MINIMUM VALUE OF THE FUNCTION IS ONE.
